tool_args.label: Nostrand Avenue / Rogers Avenue Select Bus Service Presentation to Community Board 3

payload.title: Nostrand Avenue / Rogers Avenue Select Bus Service Presentation to Community Board 3
payload.description: Presentation slides to Community Board 3 about the Nostrand Avenue / Rogers Avenue Select Bus Service project, covering corridor overview, SBS features, project design, timeline, traffic analysis, and merchant surveys.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Transit agency presenting the Nostrand Avenue / Rogers Avenue Select Bus Service project to Community Board 3.

tool_args.label: New York City Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation
payload.description: Partner agency co-presenting the Nostrand Avenue / Rogers Avenue Select Bus Service project.
Brooklyn Community Board 3, the audience for the Nostrand Avenue / Rogers Avenue Select Bus Service presentation.
Community Board referenced as having 11,000 people boarding the B44 in CB 8.

tool_args.label: Nostrand Avenue / Rogers Avenue Select Bus Service

payload.project_name: Nostrand Avenue / Rogers Avenue Select Bus Service
payload.description: SBS project on Nostrand Avenue and Rogers Avenue from Flushing Ave to Eastern Pkwy, and Empire Blvd to Farragut Rd, with bus lanes, pre-payment, bus signal priority, and station improvements.

payload.limits: from Williamsburg Bridge to Sheepshead Bay
payload.description: 9.3-mile corridor from Williamsburg Bridge to Sheepshead Bay, served by the B44 bus route.
Currently served by B44 bus route on Nostrand / Rogers corridor. SBS service planned with southbound on Nostrand Ave and northbound on Bedford Ave.
Bus signal priority as an SBS feature on the Nostrand / Rogers corridor.
Dedicated bus lanes as an SBS feature on the Nostrand / Rogers corridor. Offset bus lane on Nostrand & Rogers between Eastern Pkwy & Empire Blvd, and on Bedford Ave between DeKalb & Flushing Aves.
Off-board fare collection with MetroCard machines and coin machines at sidewalk, proof-of-payment receipt, and all-door boarding.
SBS stations with shelters, fare collection machines, bus bulbs, and higher curbs for easier boarding.
Real-time passenger information displays as an SBS feature.
Distinctive bus branding for Select Bus Service.
Inspector teams conduct random checks of buses; $100 fine for passengers without a receipt.

warnings: treatment_component.enforcement_authority is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Authority names refer to agency/entity records. Suggested relation: enforced_by.

tool_args.label: Offset Bus Lane on Nostrand & Rogers

payload.description: Offset bus lane allows buses to move quickly and preserves parking. Parking along the curb except at bus stops. Bus Lane is at right curb on Nostrand & Rogers between Eastern Pkwy & Empire Blvd, and on Bedford Ave between DeKalb & Flushing Aves.
payload.location_text: Nostrand & Rogers between Eastern Pkwy & Empire Blvd; Bedford Ave between DeKalb & Flushing Aves

normalized_location.raw_text: Nostrand & Rogers between Eastern Pkwy & Empire Blvd; Bedford Ave between DeKalb & Flushing Aves

tool_args.label: Empire Blvd SBS Station with Bus Bulb

payload.description: Sidewalks at SBS stations will extend out to the bus lane. Buses will not need to pull in and out of traffic. Higher curbs on the SBS platform will allow for easier boarding.
Commercial Vehicle delivery zones help businesses get deliveries and reduce double parking. 10am-12pm on one side of street, 12pm-2pm on other side of street – preserves critical afternoon parking.
Encourages drivers to park just as long as needed, then space is open to the next shopper. Add to commercial areas without meters.

payload.description: Buses travel at an average speed of 7-8 mph.

payload.description: 300,000 residents live within a 1/4 mile of the Nostrand / Rogers corridor.

payload.raw_value_text: 62% of households do not own a car
payload.description: 62% of households within a 1/4 mile of the corridor do not own a car.
71% of households in CB 3 do not own a car.
60% of residents within a 1/4 mile of the corridor commute by transit.
66% of residents in CB 3 commute by transit.
SBS has provided 15% to 20% faster trips on Fordham Rd and 1st/2nd Aves.
Over 90% customer satisfaction on existing SBS routes.

payload.raw_value_text: 10% ridership increase on the entire route, including SBS and local
payload.description: 10% ridership increase on the entire route, including SBS and local, on existing SBS routes.

payload.raw_value_text: 1,186 pedestrians interviewed at 4 locations on Nostrand Ave
payload.description: 1,186 pedestrians interviewed at 4 locations on Nostrand Ave for shopper survey.
55% of shoppers at Nostrand Ave & Fulton St walked from home.
19% of shoppers at Nostrand Ave & Fulton St arrived by bus.
16% of shoppers at Nostrand Ave & Fulton St arrived by subway.

tool_args.label: Shoppers who parked on Nostrand Ave

payload.description: 3% of shoppers at Nostrand Ave & Fulton St parked on Nostrand Ave.

payload.raw_value_text: Car - Not parked on Nostrand Ave 4%
payload.description: 4% of shoppers at Nostrand Ave & Fulton St arrived by car but did not park on Nostrand Ave.

payload.raw_value_text: 65% of deliveries occur midday, 10 AM to 4 PM
payload.description: 65% of deliveries occur midday, 10 AM to 4 PM.
20% of deliveries occur from 7 to 10 AM.
36% of deliveries occur from 10 AM to 1 PM.
29% of deliveries occur from 1 to 4 PM.
12% of deliveries occur from 4 to 7 PM.
16% of merchants consider customer parking most critical from 7 to 10 AM.
24% of merchants consider customer parking most critical from 10 AM to 1 PM.
25% of merchants consider customer parking most critical from 1 to 4 PM.
27% of merchants consider customer parking most critical from 4 to 7 PM.
42% of merchants prefer the existing situation for delivery zones.

tool_args.label: Merchant preference for delivery zone around corner

payload.raw_value_text: Delivery zone around the corner, all day 8%
payload.description: 8% of merchants prefer a delivery zone around the corner, all day.

tool_args.label: Merchant preference for delivery zone down block

payload.raw_value_text: A delivery zone down the block or across the street, all day 11%
payload.description: 11% of merchants prefer a delivery zone down the block or across the street, all day.

tool_args.label: Merchant preference for in-front limited hours

payload.raw_value_text: Delivery zone in front of the store, 2 or 3 hours per day 39%
payload.description: 39% of merchants prefer a delivery zone in front of the store, 2 or 3 hours per day.

payload.description: B44 is the 7th busiest bus route in New York City with 42,000 weekday riders.
